raw_body:
How I turned LLM persona interviews into a repeatable customer research workflow
The post From Ad Hoc Prompting to Repeatable AI Workflows with Claude Code Skills appeared first on Towards Data Science.
speedrun: The article discusses transforming one-time LLM persona interviews into a consistent customer research workflow using Claude Code Skills. By establishing a repeatable process, the author highlights the efficiency gained, enabling faster insights. This approach could streamline how businesses gather and analyze customer feedback, making it easier to adapt to market needs. As AI tools become more integral, refining these workflows is increasingly relevant for effective decision-making.
why_it_matters: Businesses can quickly gather and analyze customer insights, improving their responsiveness to market demands.
This shift towards structured AI workflows indicates a broader trend of integrating AI into everyday business processes for efficiency.

lenses.eli12: The article explains how to make customer research easier using AI tools. Instead of doing interviews randomly, you can set up a system that works every time. Think of it like having a recipe for a dish you love, making it easier to cook it again. This matters because it helps businesses understand their customers better and faster.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this approach means creating a reliable method for gathering customer insights. It could reduce time spent on research while increasing the quality of feedback. A practical implication is that teams can pivot their strategies based on real-time data, leading to more informed product decisions.
lenses.engineer: The article highlights using Claude Code Skills to automate LLM persona interviews, turning them into a structured workflow. This could involve specific models that enhance the consistency of data collection and analysis. While the technical details are limited, the focus is on efficiency gains through repeatability, which could be crucial for scaling customer research efforts.
